'''Joe Burgess''' (born 14 October 1994) is an English [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]] and [[England national rugby league team|England]] at international level.

He previously spent two spells with the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]], and has also played for the [[Sydney Roosters]] and the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]].

==Background==
Joe Burgess was born in [[Wigan]], [[Greater Manchester]], England. Joe played amateur rugby for [[Ince Rose Bridge]].

==Playing career==
===Wigan academy===
Burgess joined the [[Wigan Warriors]]' academy at the age of 16 from local club [[Ince Rose Bridge]]. He played a crucial part in the U19s success in the 2013 season, scoring 24 tries in just 18 appearances.<ref name="wiganwarriors" />

===Wigan Warriors===
For his efforts he was rewarded with 2 appearances in the [[Super League]] in 2013, making his début and scoring a try against [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]].<ref name="loverugbyleague.com"/>

In the 2014 season he established himself as the [[Wigan Warriors]]' first choice winger, partially because of [[Josh Charnley]]'s absence. This despite being so surprised at being asked to train with the first team at the start of the season that he made an offensive remark over the phone to coach [[Shaun Wane]], believing it to be a prank.{{citation needed|date=April 2026}} On 4 April 2014, he scored 4 tries during a 52–8 victory over [[Dewsbury Rams]]. Burgess enjoyed a fantastic rookie season for the Warriors with 22 tries (17 Super League, 4 Challenge Cup, 1 World Club Challenge) from 24 games. He also marked his first taste of Super League playoff action with a try each in all three of Wigan's playoff contests. First he scored a length of the field try in Wigan's 57-4 week one demolition of the [[Huddersfield Giants]] on 18 September 2014 after catching a high ball close to his own try line and sprinted to the opposition try line to score. Then on 3 October 2014, he would score the winning try in the dying minutes of the semi-final at home against the [[Warrington Wolves]] to break a 12–12 tie and send Wigan through to the [[2014 Super League Grand Final]] with a 16–12 win. Then in the Grand Final on 11 October 2014 against archrivals [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] he scored a try just before half time to edge Wigan 6–2 in front despite the Warriors being down to 12 men just two minutes in, but Saints would ultimately win the contest 14–6 at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/11156623/St-Helens-14-Wigan-Warriors-6-Moment-of-madness-from-Wales-international-Ben-Flower-costs-Wigan-dear.html|title= St Helens 14 Wigan Warriors 6: Moment of madness from Wales international Ben Flower costs Wigan dear|date= 11 October 2014 |work=Daily Telegraph|access-date=15 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/oct/11/st-helens-wigan-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title= St Helens win Grand Final after Wigan's Ben Flower is sent off|date= 11 October 2014 |work=Guardian |access-date=15 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.bbc.com/sport/0/rugby-league/29559845|title= St Helens 14–6 Wigan Warriors|date= 11 October 2014 |work=BBC Sport|access-date=15 October 2014}}</ref>

[[File: Joe Burgess.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Burgess playing for Wigan in 2015]]
In 2015, Burgess earned a place in the [[Super League Dream Team#2015|Super League Dream Team]].<ref name="Skysports.com">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/live/match/43791/report|title=England demolish France 84-4 in record win|publisher=Skysports.com|access-date=24 October 2015}}</ref><ref name="sportinglife.com">{{cite web|url=http://www.sportinglife.com/rugby-league/live/match-report/43791/england-v-france|title=Rugby League Scores, Fixtures & Results - Sporting Life|website=Sportinglife.com|access-date=1 August 2017|archive-date=26 October 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151026223631/http://www.sportinglife.com/rugby-league/live/match-report/43791/england-v-france|url-status=dead}}</ref>

He played in the [[2015 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Leeds pip Wigan to seal treble after brilliant, breathless Grand Final">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/oct/10/leeds-wigan-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title=Leeds pip Wigan to seal treble after brilliant, breathless Grand Final|publisher=Guardian|date=10 October 2015|access-date=8 November 2019}}</ref>

===Sydney Roosters===
[[File:Joe Burgess Wyong Roos.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Burgess playing for the [[Wyong Roos]] in 2016]]
Before the 2015 season began, Burgess confirmed he would be heading to Australia in 2016 to play in the [[National Rugby League]] with the [[Sydney Roosters]] on a three-year contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/30944496|title=Wigan Warriors: Joe Burgess to join Sydney Roosters|date=22 January 2015|access-date=1 August 2017|website=Bbc.com}}</ref> On his début, he scored both Roosters tries in their 42–10 loss to the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]]. On 31 July 2016, Burgess played one game for The [[North Sydney Bears]] in The [[Intrust Super Premiership NSW]] after he was dropped by Souths for poor form. Burgess went on to score a first half hat trick as Norths defeated Wyong 34–10.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.northsydneybears.com.au/match-report-round-twenty-one-intrust-super-premiership-bears-v-roos|title=Match Report - Round Twenty One Intrust Super Premiership Bears V Roos - North Sydney Bears|date=31 July 2016|website=Northsydneybears.com.au|access-date=1 August 2017}}</ref> Midway through the 2016 season it was announced that Burgess would be returning to Wigan in 2017.

===South Sydney Rabbitohs===
[[File:Joe Burgess North Sydney Bears.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Burgess playing for the [[North Sydney Bears]] in 2016]]
In June 2016, it was announced that Burgess had completed a mid-season loan to [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]] for the remainder of the [[2016 NRL season]]. Burgess impressed while at South Sydney scoring five tries in nine appearances. Despite interest from South Sydney to stay in Australia he decided his future lay at Wigan.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===Return to Wigan===
Burgess returned to Wigan in 2017 after the Roosters agreed to grant a release.<ref>[http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/roosters/nrl-2016-sydney-roosters-release-joe-burgess-back-to-wigan-warriors/news-story/1b9e18688b69b5d5bcb0c47f51fcc7f6 Sydney Roosters release Joe Burgess back to Wigan Warriors] ''[[Fox Sports (Australia)|Fox Sports]]'' 17 June 2016</ref>

Burgess scored a try on his second début for Wigan on 11 February against [[Salford Red Devils]] as the Warriors opened their defence of their Super League title with a 26–16 win. Burgess would also score a hat trick as Wigan won the [[World Club Challenge]] for a record fourth time as they defeated the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] 22–6 on 19 February, becoming the second player in the history of the competition to score a hat trick in the game.

He made his return for the club at [[Salford Red Devils]] in Round 1 of [[Super League]] where he scored a try thanks to a [[George Williams (rugby league, born 1994)|George Williams]] grubber kick. A week later he scored a hat-trick to help Wigan win a record breaking fourth World title. He continued his sensational start to the season with two tries against [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] before an injury in the Round 4 fixture against [[Leigh Centurions]] ruled him out for six weeks.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors/trio-injured-in-victory-against-leigh-1-8420916|title=Trio injured in victory against Leigh|website=www.wigantoday.net|language=en|access-date=2018-01-16}}</ref> He returned on Good Friday to face old rivals [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] where thanks to kicks from halfback [[George Williams (rugby league, born 1994)|Williams]] he scored a brace. Due to the injury crisis at Wigan he was forced to play at centre for several matches however that didn't affect his scoring ability as he scored another brace against the [[Catalans Dragons]] in Round 11 to take his tally to 10 for the season in all competitions.

In their first [[Challenge Cup]] fixture of the year he scored another brace of tries one from his own kick through and the other from second rower [[Liam Farrell]]'s kick. At the [[Magic Weekend]] in Newcastle he collected a high bomb from captain [[Sean O'Loughlin|O'Loughlin]] to gain a draw in a dramatic match but Williams could not convert to win the game. Round 15 saw Warriors travel to St Helens where he scored off the back of yet another Williams grubber kick. Tries in the away defeats at [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]], and Leigh Centurions followed before a brace in the Challenge Cup quarter final at [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/40265452|title=Challenge Cup quarter-final: Warrington Wolves 26-27 Wigan Warriors|date=2017-06-17|work=BBC Sport|access-date=2018-01-16|language=en-GB}}</ref>

Round 20 saw Burgess get his 19th and 20th tries of the season before scoring a hat-trick in the south of France against the Catalans Dragons. He suffered by his standards a drought and did not score for the next five matches before his 7th brace of the season in the third round of the Super 8's against Salford. His last try of the season came in the Challenge Cup final against Hull FC, his try coming as late consolation after a Liam Farrell cut out pass found him on the wing at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup-final-live-score-11054626|title=Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs|publisher=Mirror|date=26 August 2017|access-date=6 November 2019}}</ref>
[[File:Joe Burgess Wigan.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Burgess taking the field for Wigan at [[Anfield]] in 2019]]
Burgess played in the [[2020 Super League Grand Final]] which Wigan lost 8–4 against St Helens.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55072997|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan 4-8 St Helens|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

===Salford Red Devils===
On 4 December 2020, it was announced that Burgess would join [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] from the 2021 season on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55191132|title=Joe Burgess: England winger signs for Salford after leaving Wigan for second time|work=BBC Sport |access-date=18 Dec 2020}}</ref>
In round 21 of the 2022 Betfred Super League season, Burgess scored two tries for Salford in a 44–12 upset victory over [[St Helens RFC]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12207/12663029/super-league-salford-red-devils-stun-st-helens-as-they-storm-to-victory-with-eight-try-blitz/|title=Super League: Salford Red Devils stun St Helens as they storm to victory with eight-try blitz|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>
In the 2023 Super League season, Burgess played 23 matches for Salford as the club finished 7th on the table and missed the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.salfordnow.co.uk/2023/10/02/salford-red-devils-2023-season-in-review/|title=Salford Red Devils' 2023 season in review|website=www.salfordnow.co.uk|date=2 October 2023 }}</ref>

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
On 9 January 2024, Burgess joined [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] on a one-year deal ahead of the 2024 Super League season. Burgess was released by Salford after the club said his conduct was deemed "not consistent" with the clubs expected standards.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67925211|title=Hull KR sign winger after Salford Red Devils release|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 12 October 2024, Burgess played in Hull Kingston Rovers [[2024 Super League Grand Final]] loss against Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2024/oct/12/wigan-v-hull-kr-super-league-grand-final-2024-live-score-updates?page=with:block-670aaacb8f087ed1d5a8a350/|title=Wigan v Hull KR: Super League Grand Final 2024 – as it happened|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>
On 7 June 2025, Burgess played in Hull Kingston Rovers 8-6 2025 Challenge Cup final victory over Warrington. It was the clubs first major trophy in 40 years. In round 20 of the 2025 Super League season, Burgess scored four tries in Hull Kingston Rovers 74–12 victory over Salford.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cn0gnqllpxlo|title=In pictures: Hull KR Challenge Cup victory parade|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
Burgess played a crucial part in Hull Kingston Rovers 1st placed season were they won the League Leaders Shield on 18 September beating Warrington on the last game of the regular season <ref>https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c39rzxxpyjeo</ref>
On 9 October 2025, Burgess played in Hull Kingston Rovers 2025 Super League Grand Final victory over Wigan scoring two tries.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2jw497jpzo/|title=Hull KR beat Wigan in Grand Final to complete treble|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 19 February 2026, Burgess played in Hull Kingston Rovers [[World Club Challenge]] victory against [[Brisbane Broncos|Brisbane]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/live/cwy6dlgx3kyt/|title=Relive Hull KR's World Club Challenge win over Brisbane|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===International===
After the 2014 Super League season, Burgess was selected in the England team for the [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations|2014 Four Nations]] held in Australia and New Zealand however he did not play a match.

He earned another call-up to the England squad for their [[2015 New Zealand rugby league tour of Great Britain|test series against New Zealand]]. Burgess made his début for England in a test match, before the series, against [[France national rugby league team|France]] which saw Burgess score a try in England's win over their opponents.<ref name="Skysports.com"/><ref name="sportinglife.com"/> Burgess played in the first two tests of the series against New Zealand before being dropped for the third game.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Hadfield |first1=Dave |title=England vs New Zealand match report: Elliott Whitehead double helps seal series victory for Steve McNamara's side |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/england-vs-new-zealand-match-report-elliott-whitehead-double-helps-seal-series-victory-for-steve-mcnamara-s-side-a6734601.html |publisher=The Independent |date=14 November 2015}}</ref>

Burgess returned to the England side after a ten-year absence when selected for the third test against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] in the [[2025 Kangaroo tour of England|2025 series]].<ref>{{cite web |last1=Smith |first1=Emma |title=Burgess to play first England game in 10 years |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cz0xg9x4d1zo#:~:text=Joe%20Burgess%20will%20make%20his,final%20Ashes%20Test%20against%20Australia. |publisher=BBC Sport |date=6 November 2025}}</ref>
